perturbation.original_text: Horst Entertainment agrees to pay Galactcomm $0.01 (one cent) per access up to 400,000 accesses thereafter payment shall be $0.005 (one-half cent) per access.
perturbation.changed_text: Horst Entertainment agrees to pay Galacticomm $0.01 (one cent) per access up to 400,000 accesses; subsequently, the 'access fee' shall be determined by mutual agreement between both parties.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the payment terms after 400,000 accesses from a fixed rate to a 'mutually agreed' fee, it introduces ambiguity and contradicts the initial clear pricing structure. This makes enforcement uncertain as the contract no longer specifies a concrete payment amount beyond the initial threshold. It contradicts the expectation of a fixed rate established at the start of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Horst Entertainment agrees to pay Galacticomm $0.01 (one cent) per access up to 400,000 accesses; subsequently, the 'access fee' shall be determined by mutual agreement between both parties. Horst Entertainment shall send this amount to Galacticomm by no later than Wednesday for accesses used from the previuos week (Monday thru Sunday).
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces a fixed payment rate after 400,000 accesses with a fee to be determined by mutual agreement. The contradicted text then references this 'access fee' and stipulates a payment schedule based on accesses used from the previous week. Because the access fee is no longer fixed but subject to agreement, it creates a contradiction, as the payment schedule assumes a defined amount, which is no longer guaranteed after 400,000 accesses. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' as the changed text introduces ambiguity where the contradicted text expects certainty, making enforcement difficult. Additionally, it follows the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' because it alters essential language concerning payment after 400,000 accesses, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Galacticomm must provide a person(s) to correct any technical problems (Server being down or inaccessible) 24 hours per day, 7 days per week. This person(s) must be available by beeper or telephone.
perturbation.changed_text: Galacticomm will endeavor to provide support personnel to address significant technical issues (critical Server outages) during standard business hours. These support personnel can be reached through designated communication channels.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates 24/7 support with specific availability methods ('beeper or telephone'). The revised text weakens the obligation to 'endeavor to provide' during 'standard business hours' for 'critical Server outages' and refers to 'designated communication channels' introducing ambiguity. This contradicts the original guarantee of round-the-clock immediate support, creating uncertainty about the level and timing of support to be provided. 'endeavor to provide' is not an enforceable clause, which means, they can attempt but there is no way to be accountable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Galacticomm will endeavor to provide support personnel to address significant technical issues (critical Server outages) during standard business hours. These support personnel can be reached through designated communication channels. Horst Entertainment shall provide this same 24 hour service at the broadcast location.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly weakens the original commitment to 24/7 support, replacing it with an obligation to 'endeavor to provide' support during 'standard business hours.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text's expectation of continuous service. The original text specifies 'beeper or telephone', while the changed text introduces ambiguity with 'designated communication channels.' This weakens enforceability and creates a clear contradiction, making it uncertain whether 24/7 support is actually guaranteed, satisfying the conditions for 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: In the event Galacticomm, Inc. chooses to terminate this agreement, Horst Entertainment Inc. will have the right to purchase a license copy of the software in the amount of $15,000.00.
perturbation.changed_text: Should Galacticomm, Inc. elect to discontinue this agreement, Horst Entertainment Inc. may negotiate the acquisition of a software license, subject to prevailing market rates at the time of termination.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause clearly defines the right and price ($15,000.00) for purchasing a software license upon termination. The revised clause replaces 'will have the right to purchase a license copy of the software in the amount of $15,000.00' with 'may negotiate the acquisition of a software license, subject to prevailing market rates at the time of termination.' This eliminates the guaranteed right to purchase and introduces negotiation based on 'prevailing market rates,' which contradicts the initial fixed price. It creates uncertainty and potential disputes regarding the cost of the software license if Galacticomm terminates the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Should Galacticomm, Inc. elect to discontinue this agreement, Horst Entertainment Inc. may negotiate the acquisition of a software license, subject to prevailing market rates at the time of termination. All parties have read and fully agree to all terms and conditions as set forth in this Web Hosting Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text contradicts the original agreement regarding the purchase of a software license upon termination. The original text specified a fixed price of $15,000.00, while the changed text introduces a negotiation based on 'prevailing market rates'. The contradicted text is also the changed text. This creates uncertainty regarding the cost of the license and nullifies the initial fixed price, resulting in a direct conflict. As the explanation states, the revised clause contradicts the initial fixed price and is categorized under Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction, indicating a strong contradiction.
